title: Citrus oxanthera
taxonomy: Citrus oxanthera is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its fruit type and leaf morphology. As a member of the Rutaceae family, Citrus oxanthera is closely related to other citrus species, including oranges, lemons, and limes. The genus Citrus itself is a monophyletic group, meaning it consists of species that share a common ancestor. Further classification within the genus Citrus is based on the plant's botanical characteristics, such as its fruit size and seed number.

intro: Citrus oxanthera, also known as the Chinese bitter orange, is a species of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family, native to southern China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve digestion. The bioactive constituents of Citrus oxanthera include flavonoids, limonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditional Chinese medicine often utilizes Citrus oxanthera in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and cardiovascular disease.
